name: Light Chocolate Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
FILLING:
1 cup packed dark brown sugar
1/4 cup cocoa powder
1/4 cup all-purpose flour
16 ounces light cream cheese, softened
1 cup light sour cream
4 large egg whites
1 1/2 ounces sweet chocolate squares (melted and cooled)
2 teaspoons vanilla extract
OVEN: 300°F
Crust:
1. In a medium bowl, toss the cookie crumbs with granulated sugar and water until evenly moistened.
2. Press the crumb mixture into the bottom and 1/3 of the way up the sides of an 8-inch springform pan.
Filling:
1. In a small bowl, blend brown sugar, cocoa powder, and flour.
2. Process cream cheese and the flour-cocoa mixture until smooth in a food processor.
3. Add sour cream and blend until smooth.
4. Add egg whites and blend.
5. Add melted chocolate and vanilla extract and blend.
6. Pour filling into the crust and place cheesecake on the center rack of the oven.
Baking:
1. Bake for 1 hour, or until the filling is just set (it will be wobbly in the center).
2. Turn off the heat but leave the cake in the oven for another 30 minutes.
3. Remove from the oven and let cool in the pan on a wire rack. Cover and refrigerate until well chilled, at least 8 hours or overnight.
To serve:
1. Run a knife around the edges of the cake to loosen it from the side of the springform.
2. Remove the sides of the pan.
